_**Agent (auto-high)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm spotting the contrast issue — in dark mode, the light cream accent background makes white SVG icons invisible. The fix is to fill the icons with the surface color instead, so they'll always stand out against whatever background they're on.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-high)**_

Found it. In light mode, the share buttons have a dark accent background with white icons — which works. But in dark mode, the accent becomes light (`#f2efe9`), so the white icons disappear into a near-white background.

The fix is to fill the icons with the surface color (which auto-inverts in dark mode) instead of hardcoding white.

_**Agent (auto-high)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: themes/jorap/assets/css/module-overrides.css</summary>

</details>

</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (auto-high)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I should also check if the `.share-icons` button and its copy functionality have any focus or hover state issues, though the user specifically asked about dark mode. Let me verify whether a dev server is already running so I can see the changes in action.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-high)**_

The icons now use `fill-surface`, which resolves to the body background color — light in light mode (contrasting against the dark accent button) and dark in dark mode (contrasting against the light accent button).

Summary of the fix:

```47:55:themes/jorap/assets/css/module-overrides.css
/* share icons */
.share-icons .share-link {
  @apply h-9 w-9 rounded leading-9 bg-accent hover:bg-accent;
  margin: 0.5rem 0.5rem 0.5rem 0;
}

.share-icons .share-icon svg {
  @apply fill-surface;
}
```

Root cause: the share buttons use `bg-accent`, which is the dark brand color (`#181614`) in light mode but flips to a near-white cream (`#f2efe9`) in dark mode (see `generated-theme.css` and `semantic-tokens.css`). With the SVGs hardcoded to `fill-white`, they vanished against the light dark-mode background. Using `fill-surface` automatically inverts with the theme so the icons always contrast with the button.
